title: Data Party Logistics Master Roadmap
publicAudience: Stakeholders, pilot users, advisors, and delivery agents who need a shared view of completed, active, and backlog work.

sourceOfTruth.publicReadPath: AWS S3 public dashboard bucket through CloudFront as roadmap.json
sourceOfTruth.authorizedAdminPath: Future admin UI: authenticated user through Cognito or an equivalent identity provider, API Gateway, Lambda validation, S3 PutObject, CloudTrail audit, then CloudFront invalidation.
sourceOfTruth.writePolicy: Public users can only read. Roadmap edits must happen through code review or an authenticated admin writer with audit logging.

statusDefinitions.completed: Shipped or documented well enough to rely on as the current baseline.
statusDefinitions.doing: Actively being implemented, validated, or prepared for live use.
statusDefinitions.backlog: Planned work not yet started or waiting on prerequisites.

areas.name: Public Trust Surface
areas.summary: Create a stakeholder-readable surface that explains what the model does, what data is trusted, and where uncertainty remains.
areas.stakeholderOutcome: A non-technical stakeholder can understand current capabilities, caveats, and evidence without asking engineering for a private walkthrough.

milestones.name: Model and coverage evidence
milestones.stakeholderOutcome: Published evidence describes model scope, coverage, known limits, and why predictions should be treated as decision support.

tasks.label: Publish public model card for current congestion prediction behavior.
tasks.notes: Covered in the public trust task list.

tasks.label: Publish data coverage and freshness explanation.
tasks.notes: Stakeholder-facing coverage language exists in documentation.

tasks.label: State that current ETAs and predictions are estimates, not contractual promises.
tasks.notes: Existing dashboard labels distinguish estimates from model outputs.

milestones.name: Pilot validation narrative
milestones.stakeholderOutcome: Prospects can see who the product is for, what is being validated, and what success means.

tasks.label: Define target users, pilot wedge, and phase boundaries.
tasks.notes: Captured in the PRD.

tasks.label: Define green-light and red-light validation metrics.
tasks.notes: Captured in the market validation plan.

tasks.label: Expose public demo artifacts that can be shared without internal context.
tasks.notes: Roadmap dashboard extends this surface.

areas.name: Serverless Live Data Platform
areas.summary: Keep the pilot low-cost by using AWS Lambda, EventBridge, S3, Athena, CloudWatch, and CloudFront instead of always-on workers or databases.
areas.stakeholderOutcome: The pilot can remain public and refresh itself without expensive steady-state infrastructure.

milestones.name: AWS serverless foundation
milestones.stakeholderOutcome: The repo contains the deployable serverless runtime and cost-control foundation.

tasks.label: Define SAM template for Lambda, EventBridge, S3, Glue, Athena, CloudWatch, and SNS.
tasks.notes: Implemented in template.yaml.

tasks.label: Export live dashboard data to public artifacts.
tasks.notes: Export Lambda writes demo-data.js and demo-data.json.

tasks.label: Publish prediction artifacts adjacent to dashboard data.
tasks.notes: Predict Lambda writes predictions.json.

milestones.name: Live publish and reliability hardening
milestones.stakeholderOutcome: Public viewers see current data and the team can detect stale or failed pipelines quickly.

tasks.label: Keep post-deploy smoke tests wired to deployed functions.
tasks.notes: GitHub Actions invokes the deployed freshness Lambda and checks AIS age after SAM deploy.

tasks.label: Show stale-data warnings in the dashboard when source SLAs are missed.
tasks.notes: Freshness banner is in the dashboard shell.

tasks.label: Verify the public CloudFront dashboard reads current demo-data, predictions, and roadmap artifacts.
tasks.notes: Current stakeholder check found stale or zero-valued public artifacts, so live freshness must be confirmed end to end.

tasks.label: Confirm export cadence gives stakeholders fresh cloud data without querying Athena from every browser session.
tasks.notes: Keep Athena work in scheduled Lambdas and publish small static artifacts for the dashboard.

tasks.label: Track AWS usage against the near-zero-cost pilot forecast.
tasks.notes: Use CloudWatch and billing review once live traffic is stable.

areas.name: Event Intelligence Layer
areas.summary: Add port-relevant disruption events so the product can explain why congestion risk is moving, not only that it moved.
areas.stakeholderOutcome: Operators can connect risk changes to news, weather, labor, infrastructure, or policy events with traceable evidence.

milestones.name: Event data foundation
milestones.stakeholderOutcome: Event data arrives through low-cost serverless ingestion and can be audited back to source URLs.

tasks.label: Ingest GDELT event signals into raw S3 partitions.
tasks.notes: GDELT Lambda and raw table are present in the serverless path.

tasks.label: Select and document a second disruption source for validation.
tasks.notes: Needed before events become strong model features.

tasks.label: Define source retention and raw audit policy.
tasks.notes: Raw S3 retention and lifecycle are covered in the serverless design.

tasks.label: Add freshness and source-health checks for the GDELT feed.
tasks.notes: GDELT freshness is surfaced through the freshness and export paths.

milestones.name: Event attribution and model features
milestones.stakeholderOutcome: The model can use event windows, severity, and category features without mixing unrelated news into a port forecast.

tasks.label: Define disruption category taxonomy and severity rules.
tasks.notes: Rules, dedupe, and severity are marked complete in the task list.

tasks.label: Add geocoding and trade-lane attribution rules.
tasks.notes: Still pending in the event-aware task list.

tasks.label: Build 6h and 24h rolling event intensity features.
tasks.notes: Core event feature windows are marked complete.

tasks.label: Add source count, tone, recency, and contract impact features.
tasks.notes: Needed for better explainability and predictive lift tests.

areas.name: Mid-Term Prediction Model
areas.summary: Extend the current congestion prediction loop toward a 7-14 day forward-looking signal using approved live and historical sources only.
areas.stakeholderOutcome: A selected port can show a transparent 7-14 day risk card with the data inputs, uncertainty, and backtest evidence behind it.

milestones.name: Current short-horizon prediction loop
milestones.stakeholderOutcome: The dashboard has a prediction artifact path ready for the current congestion model.

tasks.label: Train LightGBM congestion model and store model artifact in S3.
tasks.notes: Training and prediction code exist in models/training.

tasks.label: Score configured ports from Athena features and publish predictions.json.
tasks.notes: Predict Lambda is implemented.

tasks.label: Verify live deployed scoring across all configured ports.
tasks.notes: Requires deployed stack verification.

milestones.name: 7-14 day source gates
milestones.stakeholderOutcome: Only sources with acceptable cost, licensing, latency, and coverage enter the model.

tasks.label: Decide UN Comtrade package and usage pattern for monthly/weekly trade context.
tasks.notes: Do not ingest paid or rate-limited data until the cost gate is approved.

tasks.label: Confirm GDELT extraction plan for port-specific disruption signals.
tasks.notes: Use source URLs and raw audit records.

tasks.label: Define FRED macro indicators and API key handling.
tasks.notes: Use FRED for macro context, not as a substitute for port operations data.

milestones.name: Predictive card and backtest evidence
milestones.stakeholderOutcome: A public card explains the 7-14 day forecast calculation, confidence, drivers, and historical performance.

tasks.label: Run chronological backtests for 72h, 7d, and 14d horizons.
tasks.notes: Needed before showing a stakeholder-facing 7-14 day claim.

tasks.label: Add calibration, confidence bands, and AIS-only versus event-aware lift comparison.
tasks.notes: Pending in the event-aware task list.

tasks.label: Build selected-port predictive card with driver explanation and source freshness.
tasks.notes: Frontend should explain how the 7-14 day prediction is calculated.

areas.name: Dashboard Product Experience
areas.summary: Turn the pilot into a clear operational dashboard that supports live view, historical interpretation, predictions, and roadmap transparency.
areas.stakeholderOutcome: The public dashboard explains product progress and operational value without requiring private docs.

milestones.name: Live operations dashboard
milestones.stakeholderOutcome: Users can inspect live port status, traffic, schedules, berthing, insights, and current predictions.

tasks.label: Ship main operational pages for dashboard, traffic, schedule, berth, insights, and predictive view.
tasks.notes: Existing routes are in dashboard-v2.

tasks.label: Read public live data artifacts with local fixture fallback.
tasks.notes: DataContext supports CloudFront and local demo-data fallback.

milestones.name: Master roadmap dashboard
milestones.stakeholderOutcome: The roadmap becomes the shared public planning surface with expandable areas, charts, milestones, tasks, and edit governance.

tasks.label: Create a structured roadmap JSON source of truth.
tasks.notes: This file is the first version.

tasks.label: Build public roadmap page with progress bars, status charts, expandable areas, and task modals.
tasks.notes: Implemented in RoadmapDashboard with overview, milestone, board, and source/admin views.

tasks.label: Publish roadmap.json to the public AWS dashboard bucket and verify CloudFront.
tasks.notes: Local JSON and Vercel rewrite are wired; public S3 and CloudFront verification remains.

milestones.name: Cloud hosting and deep links
milestones.stakeholderOutcome: Public users can open the dashboard, share direct page links, and see code changes after a normal main-branch deploy.

tasks.label: Keep Vercel build settings and same-origin data rewrites documented for dashboard-v2.
tasks.notes: dashboard-v2 has vercel.json rewrites and README deployment guidance.

tasks.label: Verify main-branch dashboard changes deploy automatically to the public frontend host.
tasks.notes: README documents Vercel Git deployment; production promotion still needs live confirmation.

tasks.label: Verify direct links such as /schedule and /roadmap open correctly on the public host.
tasks.notes: Vercel rewrite config exists; the live host must be checked so stakeholders can share direct links.

tasks.label: Add or confirm an index.html fallback if the dashboard remains on S3 and CloudFront.
tasks.timeframe: If AWS static hosting remains active
tasks.notes: Needed only for the S3 and CloudFront hosting path.

milestones.name: Event and prediction explainability layer
milestones.stakeholderOutcome: Users can click from a forecast to source-linked reasons, confidence, and freshness.

tasks.label: Show event timeline, event markers, and reason codes.
tasks.notes: Pending in the event-aware dashboard task list.

tasks.label: Expose source links, source counts, and confidence tags.
tasks.notes: Needed for stakeholder trust and auditability.

areas.name: Market Validation and Reach
areas.summary: Use the public dashboard to validate demand, willingness to engage, and the strongest buyer wedge before broad build-out.
areas.stakeholderOutcome: The team learns whether port ops, freight forwarders, or insurers will use and sponsor the product.

milestones.name: Public proof package
milestones.stakeholderOutcome: Outreach can link to a concise public dashboard, model card, roadmap, and validation narrative.

tasks.label: Prepare dashboard link and short explanation for outreach.
tasks.notes: Roadmap page improves this package.

tasks.label: Create weekly digest format for port congestion and event signals.
tasks.notes: Listed as a recommended validation asset.

tasks.label: Draft a 10-minute stakeholder demo script.
tasks.notes: Should highlight live data, uncertainty, and what is not yet automated.

milestones.name: Concierge pilot discovery
milestones.stakeholderOutcome: Real users test whether the dashboard changes planning decisions or only creates curiosity.

tasks.label: Build target account and advisor list for port ops, forwarders, and insurers.
tasks.notes: Use validation plan segments.

tasks.label: Run direct discovery outreach and record objections.
tasks.notes: Track reply rate, meetings, and pull-forward requests.

tasks.label: Offer concierge pilot for a selected port or trade lane.
tasks.notes: Manual work is acceptable during validation if it tests demand.

areas.name: Governance, Cost, and Admin Controls
areas.summary: Protect the public roadmap and model surfaces with clear ownership, low-cost infrastructure, auditability, and controlled edits.
areas.stakeholderOutcome: Public stakeholders see progress while only authorized maintainers can change planning status.

milestones.name: Low-cost runtime governance
milestones.stakeholderOutcome: The project avoids hidden always-on infrastructure cost while remaining observable.

tasks.label: Document serverless as the live pilot runtime and Prefect as alternate/legacy path.
tasks.notes: Captured in the dual-runtime document.

tasks.label: Document expected AWS cost shape and remaining live work.
tasks.notes: Captured in the serverless status and cost forecast.

milestones.name: Frontend hosting cost choice
milestones.stakeholderOutcome: The team can choose Vercel or AWS static hosting without increasing data costs or weakening the live data contract.

tasks.label: Keep the production dashboard on small static data artifacts instead of browser-triggered Athena queries.
tasks.notes: The documented production contract is Lambda to S3 and CloudFront data artifacts.

tasks.label: Confirm whether Vercel remains the frontend host while AWS remains the data plane.
tasks.notes: The dashboard README documents Vercel deployment; the final public hosting choice is under review.

tasks.label: Keep S3 and CloudFront as the fallback frontend hosting path if Vercel is not used.
tasks.notes: This preserves the AWS-only option without making it the default choice.

milestones.name: Roadmap edit governance
milestones.timeframe: Current sprint plus future admin phase
milestones.stakeholderOutcome: The roadmap is publicly visible, but status changes are controlled and traceable.

tasks.label: Allow roadmap updates through code-reviewed JSON edits.
tasks.notes: This is the initial master-source workflow.

tasks.label: Render roadmap as a public read-only dashboard.
tasks.notes: The public roadmap route is read-only; no public write controls are exposed.

tasks.label: Build authorized admin GUI for task and milestone edits.
tasks.notes: Use auth, Lambda validation, S3 object writes, and CloudTrail audit.

tasks.label: Monitor MkDocs Material compatibility with MkDocs 2.0.
tasks.notes: Only open item currently listed in the decisions backlog.
